# Author ADR 0028: temporal-pyramid summarization substrate

## Context

Epic #12679 (Temporal-Pyramid Summarization Substrate, graduated from Discussion `#11376` on 2026-06-07) names its own foundational sub: **author + accept the new ADR**; all implementation subs are merge-blocked until it is `Accepted` (per `#11376` §6 / ADR 0005 workflow). The epic has sat unowned with zero subs since graduation. The operator flagged the strategic-awareness thread (`#11375` → `#12679`) as high-ROI on 2026-07-02; the exploration pass (session 4185a2ee-ff65-4544-9cc1-049d73e56649) confirmed it and recommended filing this sub sequenced after the docking design spec — that condition is met (#14423 → PR #14425 open, in cross-family review).

**Re-verify-at-filing instruction executed:** the epic body says *"re-verify next-free at sub-filing if another ADR lands first."* Seven ADRs landed since: `0020` (its recorded next-free) is now `0020-agent-harness-concept.md`. **Live next-free = `0028`** (`0020`–`0027` all occupied; verified via `ls learn/agentos/decisions/` at 2026-07-02T03:24Z).

**Live demonstration of the drift cost:** `ask_knowledge_base` today (2026-07-02T03:24Z) answers that the pyramid's foundational ADR "**is ADR 0020**" — the stale pointer has propagated into KB synthesis and now misdirects to an unrelated accepted ADR. This is the third life of the same failure class (`0008` placeholder → `0020` correction → both stale); every week the ADR stays unfiled, the misdirection compounds.

## The Problem

The pyramid's architecture currently lives only in the graduated Discussion and the epic body — exactly the photocopy-cascade exposure the pyramid itself exists to kill. Two June-2026 empirical anchors belong in the ADR as evidence:

1. **The failure the layer prevents fired while the layer sat unbuilt:** the operator's design-first directive drifted unflagged across June's ~1005 merged PRs; no bird's-eye layer existed to surface the drift (assessed 2026-07-02: a ~500-ticket design-tier retrofit).
2. **Convergent re-derivation:** `#13441`'s freshness/authority ledger re-derived `#11375`'s `DerivedSignalContract` weeks later in different vocabulary, and `#14422`'s measurement consumers overlap `#12679`'s current-state + velocity scope. The absence of the recorded strategic layer caused partial re-derivation of the strategic layer — simultaneously the strongest ROI proof and the exact re-derivation class the substrate makes computable.

Without the accepted ADR: every impl sub stays merge-blocked, every adjacent design keeps re-deriving, and the KB keeps synthesizing the wrong authority pointer.

## The Architectural Reality

Graduated decisions the ADR records as authority (from `#12679` / `#11376`, not re-litigated here):

- **Tier split:** durable L1/L2 daily/weekly precompute (backpressure pattern per PR `#12676`) + dynamic L3–L5 synthesis (kills the photocopy cascade; incorporates the Gemini-identified fix).
- **Storage:** one temporal-summary Chroma collection + SQLite-graph-label schema — **must reconcile with ADR 0017** (`chroma-single-flat-unified-store`): whether the temporal collection is within-posture or an amendment is a decision the ADR itself must state explicitly.
- **Velocity metrics (OQ8):** deterministic structured fields at durable tiers (`mergedPrs`, `devCommits`, `sessionsPerAgent`, `highImpactSessions`, `adrsLanded`, `sandboxesGraduated`), per-field named source; prose is never the source of truth.
- **Fidelity (OQ5):** sessions `impact>=90` + accepted-ADRs direct-cited at higher tiers; PRs via a named source — no invented universal PR impact score.
- **Partitioning:** per-agent partition.

Adjacent accepted authority the ADR must position against: ADR 0005 (decision-record workflow — status lifecycle governing the `Accepted` merge-gate), ADR 0017 (store posture), ADR 0023 (DreamService consolidation/liveness), ADR 0024 (native edge graph model).

## The Fix

Author `learn/agentos/decisions/0028-temporal-pyramid-summarization-substrate.md` — sibling-lift register from ADR 0024 (data-architecture class), status conventions per ADR 0005. **One PR.** The ADR contains: the decision set above; an explicit ADR-0017 reconciliation clause; the June empirical anchors as evidence; revalidation triggers; the impl-merge-gate restatement.

No new `.mjs` (structural-pre-flight stays cold). Nav registration in `learn/tree.json` is optional per live precedent (4 of 27 ADRs registered); default: unregistered, decided at authoring.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `learn/agentos/decisions/0028-temporal-pyramid-summarization-substrate.md` (new) | `#12679` graduated decision + `#11376` §6 + ADR 0005 workflow | Records pyramid authority; anchors the impl-sub merge gate (`Accepted` required) | Epic body remains interim authority (status quo) | The ADR IS the docs | Cross-family-reviewed PR |
| ADR 0017 relationship | ADR 0017 accepted authority | ADR 0028 declares within-posture OR names an explicit amendment clause — never silent divergence | — | ADR 0028 reconciliation section | Reviewer verifies the clause against ADR 0017 |
| `#12679` in-body next-free pointer | The epic's own re-verify-at-sub-filing instruction | Pinned to `0028` by this ticket; disposed via comment on the epic (no body edit — authorship respect, Ada's artifact) | — | This ticket + native sub-link | `ls decisions/` verification recorded above |

## Decision Record

Required: **ADR 0028** (this ticket's deliverable) — executes the epic's correction chain (`#11376` `ADR 0008` placeholder → epic-review `0020` → filing-time `0028`).

## Decision Record impact

`depends-on ADR 0005` (workflow/status lifecycle) · `aligned-with ADR 0023, ADR 0024` · `aligned-with-or-amends ADR 0017` — the specific 0017 relationship is settled inside the ADR with an explicit clause (never silent).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ADR 0028 authored + merged: decision statement (L1/L2-durable vs L3–L5-dynamic split), storage posture with explicit ADR-0017 reconciliation clause, deterministic velocity-field table with per-field named sources, fidelity/citation discipline (impact≥90 + accepted-ADR direct-citation), per-agent partition, revalidation triggers.
- [ ] Status lifecycle per ADR 0005; the impl-sub merge gate (`Accepted` required) restated in the ADR.
- [ ] June-2026 empirical anchors folded as cited evidence (design-first drift; convergent re-derivation `#13441` / `#14422`).
- [ ] Next-free verification recorded in the ADR header/provenance (0028; `0020`–`0027` occupied at 2026-07-02).
- [ ] Native SUB_ISSUE link under #12679; the epic's stale in-body `0020` pointer disposed via comment referencing this sub.
- [ ] Cross-family review from ≥1 non-fable family before merge.
- [ ] `learn/tree.json` registration decision recorded (default: unregistered, majority precedent).

## Out of Scope

- Implementing any pipeline tier (L1–L5 lanes are sibling subs, merge-blocked until the ADR is `Accepted`).
- The `#14422` authority-vocabulary unification (that Discussion's divergence window owns it; the ADR may cite it as adjacent).
- The chronicle-as-L4/L5-prototype (separate narrative-lane artifact; consumes the ADR, doesn't define it).
- Re-litigating graduated `#11376` decisions — the ADR records them; changes flow through its own amendment process.

## Avoided Traps

- **Authoring without the ADR-0017 reconciliation** — rejected: silent divergence would create dueling store-posture authorities, the exact class of ambiguity ADRs exist to kill.
- **Carrying any stale number forward** — rejected: `0008` → `0020` → (now) `0028` is a recurrence class; this filing terminates it by pinning at filing time with recorded verification, per the epic's own instruction.
- **Treating the ADR as the epic's decomposition** — rejected: subs are filed separately per `epic-create`; the ADR is authority, not a work plan.
- **Waiting for operator go-ahead** — rejected: merge is the only human gate; design-first substrate is peer-convergence work the swarm drives.

## Related

Parent: #12679. Source Discussion: `#11376` (graduated 2026-06-07). Sequenced after: `#14423` (PR `#14425`, in review). Evidence-adjacent: `#11375`, `#13441`, `#14422`. Authority-adjacent: ADR 0005 / 0017 / 0023 / 0024.
